warnings: Only 1 monitored bed free — protect for this patient
Patient on apixaban: hemorrhagic stroke must be ruled out before any thrombolytic consideration
Do NOT lower BP unless >220/120 per stroke protocol

hypotheses.name: Acute ischemic stroke (LVO possible)
supporting_signals: sudden focal deficit: right facial droop, right arm weakness, slurred speech
last-known-well 40 min ago — within 4.5h tPA window

hypotheses.name: Hypoglycemia / metabolic mimic
rules_against: fingerstick not yet done — must rule out

immediate.action: Activate stroke team; CT head non-contrast STAT + CTA head/neck
immediate.reason: Focal deficit + LKW 40 min — within tPA/thrombectomy window; must distinguish ischemic vs hemorrhagic given apixaban.

constraints_checked.result: on apixaban — DOAC reversal (andexanet/4F-PCC) on standby if ICH

immediate.action: Place in monitored bed, IV x2, fingerstick glucose, NIHSS, labs (CBC, BMP, coags, type & screen)
immediate.reason: Stroke workup bundle; fingerstick rules out hypoglycemic mimic before imaging.

immediate.action: Hold antihypertensives; do NOT lower BP unless >220/120
immediate.reason: Per stroke protocol BP 162/94 is permissive; lowering risks penumbra hypoperfusion.

monitor.action: Serial neuro checks q15 min and continuous cardiac monitoring (AFib)
monitor.reason: Detect deterioration / hemorrhagic conversion; AFib rhythm surveillance.

monitor.action: Track CT result and coag panel turnaround
monitor.reason: Drives tPA/thrombectomy vs reversal decision branch.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Page neurology now (ETA 25 min) and alert interventional/thrombectomy-capable center
escalate_or_redirect.reason: Stroke decision is specialty-driven; possible LVO transfer if CTA confirms.

explanation.summary: 71M on apixaban with sudden right-sided facial droop, right arm weakness, slurred speech, confusion; LKW 40 min ago. Classic acute stroke presentation within tPA/thrombectomy window. ESI 1 — stroke alert. DOAC complicates thrombolysis and raises ICH probability; CT head STAT is the pivot. BP permissive at 162/94. Vitals otherwise stable. Protect the one free monitored bed for this patient.

citations.signal: sudden focal deficit (face droop, arm weakness, slurred speech) + LKW 40 min
patient on apixaban — CT head STAT regardless, ICH must be excluded

checklist.task: Stroke alert activation + CT scanner cleared
CT head non-contrast (and CTA head/neck) completed within 25 min of arrival
Fingerstick glucose, NIHSS documented, two IVs, stroke labs drawn
Confirm last apixaban dose timing with wife (anti-Xa level if <15h)
Reassess neuro exam q15 min until disposition
Identify thrombectomy-capable transfer destination if LVO

why_this_tier: Acute focal neuro deficit within tPA window
Anticoagulated — ICH risk demands STAT imaging
Confusion/AMS meets ESI 2 high-risk criterion independently
would_upgrade_if: GCS drop, new vomiting, BP >220/120, or airway compromise (already at top tier)
would_downgrade_if: Symptoms fully resolved (TIA) AND CT/CTA negative AND glucose normal — still stays 'now' for stroke workup, would not actually downgrade in ED
